title: Lift SSE tail-tolerance to readEventLedger; doctor partial-write fixable
description: events.ts:363-401 SSE incremental reader already handles trailing partial line via state.eventLedgerRemainder. Lift this pattern into the full readEventLedger path (currently silently drops bad lines). Introduce LedgerWarning[] consumed by doctor. New fixable doctor check `event_ledger_partial_write` calls truncateLedgerToLastNewline helper to recover. Per pressure-pass refinement (Round 2): no hard fsync per append — that's R3 signal handler's job.
scope: Layer 2 P0 — ledger durability consumer task

files.change: Refactor readEventLedger to mirror events.ts:363-401 tail-tolerance: track remainder via streaming line buffer; if final partial line exists, surface as LedgerWarning instead of silent drop
Add export type LedgerWarning = { kind: 'partial_write_at_tail', byte_offset, byte_length, snippet_first_120 }
Add fixable check `event_ledger_partial_write` consuming LedgerWarning[]; --fix calls truncateLedgerToLastNewline (renames truncated bytes to .corrupted file before truncate for forensics)
Add tests: trailing partial line surfaces warning; truncate helper preserves bytes to .corrupted; doctor check fires

criteria: readEventLedger never silently drops trailing partial lines
doctor `event_ledger_partial_write` check is fixable; --fix truncates and writes event_ledger_truncated ledger event with byte range
Truncated bytes preserved as <ledger>.corrupted.<ts> for forensics
